bibliography:
"Low-Code/No-Code: Citizen Developer and the Surging 4th Industrial Revolution" por Phil Simon

Ano: 2021; Editora: Motion Publishing; ISBN: 978-0972488968

Link: https://www.amazon.com/Low-Code-No-Code-Citizen-Developer-Industrial-Revolution/dp/0972488962
methodology:
The teaching and learning methodology of this course is active, focusing on the student, who is the main responsible for their learning process. There is a clear incentive for the development of the ability to absorb content autonomously and participatively, through case analyses, promotion of digital content, and critical discussion of the same, supporting the theoretical content taught.

Additionally, students are challenged to carry out individual and group projects, with the aim of being presented and thus solidifying the knowledge acquired.
objectives:
Objectives of the Course:

OA1. Understand the fundamental concepts of Low Code and No Code.

OA2. Learn about the OutSystems platform and its main functionalities.

OA3. Develop the ability to model, develop, and automate business processes using OutSystems.

OA4. Explore the advantages and challenges of Low Code development.

OA5. Integrate applications developed with PowerApps to other systems and services.
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
This course includes the following programmatic content (CPs):


	CP1: Framework and Motivation; 
	CP2: Fundamentals of Low Code; 
	CP3: Introduction to PowerApps
	CP4: Business Process Modeling with BPMN
	CP5: Development with PowerApps
	CP6: Integration and Extensibility
	CP7: Process Automation
	CP8: Best Practices and Governance
	CP9: Case Studies and Practical Applications
presentation: Introduction to the main concepts and skills in Low Code and No Code development, enabling students to use the PowerApps platform to model, develop, and automate business processes. Focus on the advantages, challenges, and best practices for Low Code development, with integration to external systems and practical application in real projects, preparing students to meet the technological needs of modern organizations.
